pattern: Grouping every candidate value in range by its digit sum turns the search into a classic "count non-decreasing sequences" DP over value, and because the value range is small and fixed, a running prefix count over it keeps every step cheap.
hints: First figure out, for every digit sum that could possibly appear, the full sorted list of values in the allowed range achieving it -- most digit sums beyond a certain size simply have no matching value at all.

Process positions left to right. For each position, only the values matching its required digit sum are candidates, and each candidate's count of ways is the total number of ways to have ended at any earlier value no greater than it.

That "total ways ending at any value up to v" is just a running prefix sum over the previous position's per-value counts -- recompute that prefix sum once per position instead of comparing every pair of candidate values.

edge_inputs.description: single position, smallest possible digit sum
digit sum with no matching value in range at all
an achievable digit sum followed by one that has no match
two positions where the second's required digit sum forces exactly one continuation
digit sum zero repeated -- only the value zero itself can ever match
decreasing digit sums that still might have increasing value continuations

stress.description: many positions with small achievable digit sums
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    digitSum = [rng.randint(0, 15) for _ in range(n)]
    return [digitSum]
